description: Deponent verb meaning to refuse or deny something. Used when declining requests or rejecting claims.
examples: Αρνούμαι να πάω. (I refuse to go.) / Αρνείται την αλήθεια. (He denies the truth.)

description: Deponent verb meaning to accept or agree to something. Formal register.
examples: Αποδέχομαι την πρόταση. (I accept the proposal.) / Αποδέχεται τους όρους. (He accepts the terms.)

description: Deponent verb meaning to respect or have regard for someone or something.
examples: Σέβομαι τους γονείς μου. (I respect my parents.) / Σεβόμαστε τους κανόνες. (We respect the rules.)

description: Deponent verb meaning to sit down or be sitting. Very common in everyday speech.
examples: Κάθομαι στον καναπέ. (I'm sitting on the sofa.) / Κάτσε εδώ! (Sit here!)

description: Active verb meaning to prefer. Takes να + verb or a noun as its object.
examples: Προτιμώ τον καφέ. (I prefer coffee.) / Προτιμώ να μείνω σπίτι. (I prefer to stay home.)

description: Deponent verb meaning to work or be employed. More formal than δουλεύω.
examples: Εργάζομαι σε τράπεζα. (I work at a bank.) / Εργάζεται πολύ σκληρά. (She works very hard.)

description: Deponent verb meaning to think about or consider something.
examples: Σκέφτομαι το μέλλον. (I'm thinking about the future.) / Τι σκέφτεσαι; (What are you thinking?)

description: Deponent verb meaning to accept or receive. Can refer to accepting guests or receiving items.
examples: Δέχομαι το δώρο. (I accept the gift.) / Δεν δέχεται επισκέψεις. (He doesn't accept visitors.)

description: Deponent verb meaning to imagine or fancy. Used for hypothetical thinking.
examples: Φαντάζομαι ένα καλύτερο κόσμο. (I imagine a better world.) / Φαντάσου! (Imagine!)

description: Deponent verb meaning to dream, both literally while sleeping and figuratively.
examples: Ονειρεύομαι κάθε νύχτα. (I dream every night.) / Ονειρεύομαι ένα ταξίδι. (I dream of a trip.)

description: Deponent verb meaning to rest or relax. Reflexive sense of resting oneself.
examples: Ξεκουράζομαι το Σαββατοκύριακο. (I rest on the weekend.) / Πρέπει να ξεκουραστείς. (You need to rest.)

description: Deponent verb meaning to become or happen. Extremely common and versatile.
examples: Γίνομαι καλύτερος. (I'm becoming better.) / Τι γίνεται; (What's happening?)

description: Deponent verb meaning to visit a place or person. More formal register.
examples: Επισκέπτομαι τη γιαγιά μου. (I visit my grandmother.) / Επισκεφτήκαμε το μουσείο. (We visited the museum.)

description: Deponent verb meaning to seem or appear. Used for impressions and appearances.
examples: Φαίνεται κουρασμένος. (He seems tired.) / Φαίνεται ωραίο. (It looks nice.)

description: Deponent verb meaning to promise. Takes να + verb or a noun.
examples: Υπόσχομαι να έρθω. (I promise to come.) / Μου υποσχέθηκε βοήθεια. (He promised me help.)

description: Deponent verb meaning to wish or hope for something. Common in greetings and blessings.
examples: Σου εύχομαι καλή τύχη! (I wish you good luck!) / Εύχομαι να πάνε όλα καλά. (I wish everything goes well.)

description: Deponent verb meaning to trust or confide in someone.
examples: Εμπιστεύομαι τον φίλο μου. (I trust my friend.) / Δεν τον εμπιστεύεται. (She doesn't trust him.)

description: Deponent verb meaning to sleep or be sleeping. Very common everyday word.
examples: Κοιμάμαι νωρίς. (I sleep early.) / Το μωρό κοιμάται. (The baby is sleeping.)

description: Deponent verb meaning to come or arrive. One of the most fundamental Greek verbs.
examples: Έρχομαι αύριο. (I'm coming tomorrow.) / Έλα εδώ! (Come here!)

description: Deponent verb meaning to be bored or fed up with something.
examples: Βαριέμαι πολύ. (I'm very bored.) / Βαρέθηκα να περιμένω. (I got tired of waiting.)

description: Deponent verb meaning to need something. Very frequently used.
examples: Χρειάζομαι βοήθεια. (I need help.) / Τι χρειάζεσαι; (What do you need?)

description: Deponent verb meaning to remember or recall something.
examples: Θυμάμαι εκείνη τη μέρα. (I remember that day.) / Δεν θυμάσαι; (Don't you remember?)

description: Active verb meaning to understand or comprehend.
examples: Καταλαβαίνω ελληνικά. (I understand Greek.) / Δεν καταλαβαίνω. (I don't understand.)

description: Active verb meaning to know or recognise someone or something.
examples: Γνωρίζω τον Γιάννη. (I know Yiannis.) / Τη γνωρίζεις; (Do you know her?)

description: Active verb meaning to wash. Can be used for dishes, clothes, hands, etc.
examples: Πλένω τα πιάτα. (I wash the dishes.) / Πλύνε τα χέρια σου! (Wash your hands!)

description: Active verb meaning to win a game/competition or earn money.
examples: Κερδίζω τον αγώνα. (I win the match.) / Κέρδισε πολλά χρήματα. (He earned a lot of money.)

description: Active verb meaning to catch or grab something. Also means to begin (πιάνω δουλειά).
examples: Πιάνω τη μπάλα. (I catch the ball.) / Πιάνω δουλειά στις 9. (I start work at 9.)

description: Active verb meaning to meet someone, either by arrangement or by chance.
examples: Συναντώ τον φίλο μου. (I meet my friend.) / Πού θα συναντηθούμε; (Where shall we meet?)

description: Active verb meaning to fill something up or become full.
examples: Γεμίζω το ποτήρι. (I fill the glass.) / Το δωμάτιο γέμισε κόσμο. (The room filled with people.)

description: Active verb meaning to hurt or be in pain. Can be transitive or intransitive.
examples: Πονάω πολύ. (I'm in a lot of pain.) / Με πονάει το κεφάλι. (My head hurts.)

description: Active verb meaning to pass by or go through. Also means to have a good time (περνάω καλά).
examples: Περνώ από το σπίτι σου. (I pass by your house.) / Περνάμε καλά! (We're having a good time!)

description: Active verb meaning to go up, climb, or ascend.
examples: Ανεβαίνω τις σκάλες. (I go up the stairs.) / Οι τιμές ανεβαίνουν. (Prices are going up.)

description: Active verb meaning to spend money or resources.
examples: Ξοδεύω πολλά λεφτά. (I spend a lot of money.) / Μην ξοδεύεις τόσα! (Don't spend so much!)

description: Active verb meaning to continue or keep going with something.
examples: Συνεχίζω τη δουλειά μου. (I continue my work.) / Συνέχισε! (Keep going!)

description: Active verb meaning to hold or keep something. Also means to last/endure.
examples: Κρατάω το βιβλίο. (I hold the book.) / Κράτα αυτό! (Keep this!)

description: Active verb meaning to support or advocate for someone or something.
examples: Υποστηρίζω την ομάδα μου. (I support my team.) / Τον υποστηρίζει πάντα. (She always supports him.)

description: Active verb meaning to go down, descend, or come downstairs.
examples: Κατεβαίνω τις σκάλες. (I go down the stairs.) / Κατέβα κάτω! (Come downstairs!)

description: Active verb meaning to reply or answer a question.
examples: Απαντώ στην ερώτηση. (I answer the question.) / Δεν απάντησε. (He didn't reply.)

description: Active verb meaning to recognise or identify someone or something.
examples: Δεν τον αναγνωρίζω. (I don't recognise him.) / Αναγνώρισε τη φωνή μου. (He recognised my voice.)

description: Active verb meaning to cut something with a knife, scissors, etc.
examples: Κόβω το ψωμί. (I cut the bread.) / Κόψε το σε κομμάτια. (Cut it into pieces.)

description: Active verb meaning to sell goods or property.
examples: Πουλάω το αυτοκίνητό μου. (I'm selling my car.) / Πουλάει φρούτα. (He sells fruit.)

description: Active verb meaning to try or attempt to do something. Takes να + verb.
examples: Προσπαθώ να μάθω ελληνικά. (I'm trying to learn Greek.) / Προσπάθησε ξανά! (Try again!)

description: Active verb meaning to order food, products, etc.
examples: Παραγγέλνω πίτσα. (I'm ordering pizza.) / Τι θα παραγγείλεις; (What will you order?)

description: Active verb meaning to start or begin something, or to set off on a journey.
examples: Ξεκινώ δουλειά αύριο. (I start work tomorrow.) / Ξεκίνησε η ταινία. (The movie has started.)

description: Pronoun/determiner used as a placeholder for an unspecified person or thing.
examples: Ο τάδε μου είπε... (So-and-so told me...) / Στην τάδε διεύθυνση. (At such-and-such address.)

description: Pronoun meaning nothing or not anything. In questions can mean 'anything'.
examples: Δεν θέλω τίποτα. (I don't want anything.) / Θέλεις τίποτα; (Do you want anything?)

description: Feminine form of κανένας meaning no one, none, or not any.
examples: Δεν έχω καμία ιδέα. (I have no idea.) / Καμία φορά πάω σινεμά. (Sometimes I go to the cinema.)

description: Pronoun meaning each one or everyone. Emphasises individuality.
examples: Καθένας έχει τη γνώμη του. (Everyone has their own opinion.) / Καθένας μας είναι διαφορετικός. (Each of us is different.)

description: Neuter form of κανένας meaning any, none, or nobody.
examples: Δεν έχω κανένα πρόβλημα. (I have no problem.) / Κανένα παιδί δεν ήρθε. (No child came.)

description: Feminine form of άλλος meaning other or another.
examples: Θέλω μια άλλη μέρα. (I want another day.) / Η άλλη κοπέλα. (The other girl.)

description: Masculine form meaning other or else. Very common determiner/pronoun.
examples: Κάποιος άλλος θα έρθει. (Someone else will come.) / Ο άλλος δρόμος. (The other road.)

description: Neuter form of άλλος. Also used idiomatically: δεν αντέχω άλλο (I can't take it anymore).
examples: Θέλεις κάτι άλλο; (Do you want something else?) / Δεν αντέχω άλλο! (I can't take anymore!)

description: Pronoun meaning something. Very frequently used in everyday speech.
examples: Θέλω να σου πω κάτι. (I want to tell you something.) / Κάτι γίνεται. (Something is going on.)

description: Determiner meaning every or each. Indeclinable — same form for all genders.
examples: Κάθε μέρα. (Every day.) / Κάθε άνθρωπος. (Every person.)

description: Pronoun meaning anyone (in questions) or nobody (with negation).
examples: Κανείς δεν ξέρει. (Nobody knows.) / Ήρθε κανείς; (Did anyone come?)

description: Feminine form of κάποιος meaning someone or some.
examples: Κάποια γυναίκα ρώτησε για σένα. (Some woman asked about you.)

description: Masculine pronoun meaning someone or somebody.
examples: Κάποιος χτυπάει την πόρτα. (Someone is knocking on the door.) / Κάποιος θα βοηθήσει. (Someone will help.)

description: Neuter form of κάποιος meaning some or a certain.
examples: Κάποιο λάθος έγινε. (Some mistake was made.) / Κάποιο πρωί. (Some morning.)

description: Feminine noun meaning day. Everyday alternative to the more formal ημέρα.
examples: Καλή μέρα! (Good day!) / Κάθε μέρα μαθαίνω κάτι νέο. (Every day I learn something new.)

description: Plural masculine adjective/pronoun meaning some or a few.
examples: Μερικοί άνθρωποι είναι ευγενικοί. (Some people are kind.) / Μερικοί ήρθαν αργά. (Some came late.)

description: Active verb meaning to help or assist someone.
examples: Βοηθάω τη μητέρα μου. (I help my mother.) / Μπορείς να με βοηθήσεις; (Can you help me?)

description: Active verb meaning to finish or complete something.
examples: Τελειώνω τη δουλειά μου. (I finish my work.) / Τελείωσε το φαγητό! (Finish your food!)

description: Active verb meaning to live or be alive. Short but very common.
examples: Ζω στην Αθήνα. (I live in Athens.) / Ζει μόνος του. (He lives alone.)

description: Negative particle used with subjunctive to express prohibition or negative wishes.
examples: Μην τρέχεις! (Don't run!) / Μην ανησυχείς. (Don't worry.)

description: Impersonal verb meaning must or have to. Always used in third person.
examples: Πρέπει να φύγω. (I must leave.) / Πρέπει να είσαι προσεκτικός. (You must be careful.)

description: Adjective meaning wide or broad. Describes physical width.
examples: Ο δρόμος είναι φαρδύς. (The road is wide.) / Φαρδιά ώμοι. (Broad shoulders.)

description: Feminine noun meaning dimension or measurement. Also used figuratively.
examples: Ποιες είναι οι διαστάσεις; (What are the dimensions?) / Μια άλλη διάσταση. (Another dimension.)

description: Adjective meaning light (not heavy). Opposite of βαρύς.
examples: Η τσάντα είναι ελαφριά. (The bag is light.) / Ελαφρύ γεύμα. (A light meal.)

description: Adjective meaning heavy. Also used figuratively for serious or harsh.
examples: Η βαλίτσα είναι βαριά. (The suitcase is heavy.) / Βαριά ατμόσφαιρα. (Heavy atmosphere.)

description: Neuter noun meaning liter, a unit of volume measurement.
examples: Ένα λίτρο γάλα. (One liter of milk.) / Δύο λίτρα νερό. (Two liters of water.)

description: Masculine noun meaning volume (capacity) or bulk/mass.
examples: Ο όγκος του δωματίου. (The volume of the room.) / Μεγάλος όγκος δεδομένων. (Large volume of data.)

description: Adjective meaning long (in length). Used for physical objects and time.
examples: Μακρύ ταξίδι. (Long journey.) / Έχει μακριά μαλλιά. (She has long hair.)

description: Adjective meaning triple or threefold.
examples: Τριπλή απειλή. (Triple threat.) / Τριπλό εσπρέσο. (Triple espresso.)

description: Active verb meaning to weigh something on a scale.
examples: Ζυγίζω τα φρούτα. (I weigh the fruit.) / Πόσο ζυγίζεις; (How much do you weigh?)

description: Neuter noun meaning weight or burden. Used both literally and figuratively.
examples: Το βάρος της βαλίτσας. (The weight of the suitcase.) / Νιώθω ένα βάρος. (I feel a burden.)

description: Neuter noun meaning gram, a unit of weight measurement.
examples: Διακόσια γραμμάρια τυρί. (Two hundred grams of cheese.)

description: Active verb meaning to count or measure something.
examples: Μετράω τα χρήματα. (I count the money.) / Μετράει τα βήματά του. (He counts his steps.)

description: Masculine noun meaning ton/tonne (weight). Also means tone or tuna fish.
examples: Ένας τόνος σίδερο. (A ton of iron.) / Τόνοι σκουπιδιών. (Tons of rubbish.)

description: Adjective meaning double or twofold.
examples: Διπλό κρεβάτι. (Double bed.) / Διπλή μερίδα. (Double portion.)

description: Neuter noun meaning meter (unit) or measure. Also refers to the metro/subway.
examples: Δύο μέτρα ύφασμα. (Two meters of fabric.) / Πάμε με το μετρό. (Let's take the metro.)

description: Neuter noun meaning width or breadth.
examples: Το πλάτος του δρόμου. (The width of the road.) / Τρία μέτρα πλάτος. (Three meters wide.)

description: Neuter noun meaning mile, a unit of distance.
examples: Πέντε μίλια μακριά. (Five miles away.) / Ναυτικό μίλι. (Nautical mile.)

description: Neuter noun meaning length.
examples: Το μήκος του δωματίου. (The length of the room.) / Δέκα μέτρα μήκος. (Ten meters in length.)

description: Neuter noun meaning centimeter or hundredth.
examples: Πέντε εκατοστά. (Five centimeters.) / Ένα εκατοστό του δευτερολέπτου. (A hundredth of a second.)

description: Neuter noun meaning height or altitude.
examples: Τι ύψος έχεις; (How tall are you?) / Σε μεγάλο ύψος. (At a great height.)

description: Preposition meaning per or each. Used in rates and distributions.
examples: Ανά ώρα. (Per hour.) / Ανά δύο. (In pairs / two at a time.)

description: Neuter noun meaning a couple or a pair of something.
examples: Ένα ζευγάρι παπούτσια. (A pair of shoes.) / Ένα νέο ζευγάρι. (A new couple.)

description: Adjective meaning average or middle. Used in statistics and general descriptions.
examples: Μέσος όρος. (Average.) / Η μέση ηλικία. (The average age.)

description: Adjective meaning enough or sufficient. Also means quite a lot.
examples: Αρκετός χρόνος. (Enough time.) / Αρκετοί άνθρωποι ήρθαν. (Quite a few people came.)

description: Adjective meaning huge, enormous, or massive.
examples: Τεράστιο σπίτι. (Huge house.) / Τεράστια πρόοδος. (Enormous progress.)

description: Feminine noun meaning majority. Used in political and general contexts.
examples: Η πλειοψηφία ψήφισε ναι. (The majority voted yes.) / Στην πλειοψηφία τους. (In their majority.)

description: Adjective meaning little or few. Very common quantifier.
examples: Λίγο νερό. (A little water.) / Λίγοι μαθητές ήρθαν. (Few students came.)

description: Adverb/adjective meaning more. Comparative form.
examples: Θέλω περισσότερο χρόνο. (I want more time.) / Περισσότεροι άνθρωποι. (More people.)

description: Adjective/noun meaning half.
examples: Μισή ώρα. (Half an hour.) / Ένα μισό κιλό. (Half a kilo.)

description: Neuter noun meaning sum or amount, typically of money.
examples: Ένα μεγάλο ποσό. (A large sum.) / Το ποσό που χρωστάω. (The amount I owe.)

description: Adverb/adjective meaning less or least. Comparative form of λίγος.
examples: Λιγότερο ζάχαρη. (Less sugar.) / Λιγότεροι τουρίστες φέτος. (Fewer tourists this year.)

description: Active verb meaning to dry something or to dry off.
examples: Στεγνώνω τα ρούχα. (I dry the clothes.) / Στεγνώνω τα χέρια μου. (I dry my hands.)

description: Impersonal verb meaning it happens or is going on. Third person only.
examples: Τι συμβαίνει; (What's happening?) / Αυτό συμβαίνει συχνά. (This happens often.)

description: Active verb meaning to improve or make better.
examples: Βελτιώνω τα ελληνικά μου. (I improve my Greek.) / Η κατάσταση βελτιώνεται. (The situation is improving.)

description: Active verb meaning to deserve or to be worth something.
examples: Αξίζει τον κόπο. (It's worth the effort.) / Αξίζεις το καλύτερο. (You deserve the best.)

description: Active verb meaning to leave or depart. One of the most basic Greek verbs.
examples: Φεύγω τώρα. (I'm leaving now.) / Πότε φεύγεις; (When are you leaving?)

description: Active verb meaning to fly. Also means to throw away.
examples: Το πουλί πετάει. (The bird is flying.) / Πέταξε τα σκουπίδια. (Throw away the rubbish.)

description: Active verb meaning to sign a document or agreement.
examples: Υπογράφω το συμβόλαιο. (I sign the contract.) / Υπέγραψε εδώ. (Sign here.)

description: Impersonal verb meaning to care about. Used with με/σε + accusative.
examples: Δεν με νοιάζει. (I don't care.) / Σε νοιάζει; (Do you care?)

description: Active verb meaning to follow someone or something.
examples: Ακολούθα με! (Follow me!) / Ακολουθώ τους κανόνες. (I follow the rules.)

description: Active verb meaning to believe in something or someone.
examples: Πιστεύω σε σένα. (I believe in you.) / Δεν πιστεύω! (I don't believe it!)

description: Active verb meaning to fail at something or be unsuccessful.
examples: Αποτύχαμε στις εξετάσεις. (We failed the exams.) / Μην φοβάσαι να αποτύχεις. (Don't be afraid to fail.)

description: Active verb meaning to lose something or miss an opportunity/transport.
examples: Έχασα το κλειδί μου. (I lost my key.) / Μην χάσεις το λεωφορείο! (Don't miss the bus!)

description: Active verb meaning to raise or bring up children.
examples: Ανατρέφω τα παιδιά μου. (I raise my children.) / Τον ανέθρεψε η γιαγιά του. (His grandmother raised him.)

description: Active verb meaning to pay attention or be careful. Also can mean to babysit.
examples: Πρόσεχε! (Be careful!) / Πρόσεχε τα σκαλιά. (Watch the steps.)

description: Active verb meaning to enter or go into a place.
examples: Μπαίνω στο σπίτι. (I enter the house.) / Μπες μέσα! (Come inside!)

description: Active verb meaning to look for or search for something.
examples: Ψάχνω τα κλειδιά μου. (I'm looking for my keys.) / Τι ψάχνεις; (What are you looking for?)

description: Active verb meaning to stop doing something or to halt.
examples: Σταμάτα! (Stop!) / Σταματάω να καπνίζω. (I stop smoking.)

description: Active verb meaning to allow or permit something.
examples: Επιτρέπεται; (Is it allowed?) / Δεν μου επιτρέπει. (He doesn't allow me.)

description: Active verb meaning to receive or collect a delivery or package.
examples: Παραλαμβάνω το δέμα. (I collect the package.) / Πού παραλαμβάνω τα εισιτήρια; (Where do I collect the tickets?)

description: Active verb meaning to hope for something. Takes να + verb.
examples: Ελπίζω να πάνε όλα καλά. (I hope everything goes well.) / Ελπίζω να σε δω σύντομα. (I hope to see you soon.)

description: Active verb meaning to close or shut something. Also means to book/reserve.
examples: Κλείνω την πόρτα. (I close the door.) / Κλείνω ένα τραπέζι. (I book a table.)

description: Active verb meaning to doubt or be uncertain about something.
examples: Αμφιβάλλω γι' αυτό. (I doubt that.) / Δεν αμφιβάλλω. (I have no doubt.)

description: Active verb meaning to go out or come out. Very common in everyday speech.
examples: Βγαίνω έξω. (I go outside.) / Βγαίνουμε απόψε; (Shall we go out tonight?)

description: Active verb meaning to ask a question.
examples: Ρωτάω μια ερώτηση. (I ask a question.) / Μπορώ να ρωτήσω κάτι; (Can I ask something?)

description: Active verb meaning to present or introduce something formally.
examples: Παρουσιάζω το σχέδιο. (I present the plan.) / Παρουσίασε τον εαυτό σου. (Introduce yourself.)

description: Active verb meaning to feel an emotion or physical sensation.
examples: Νιώθω χαρά. (I feel joy.) / Πώς νιώθεις; (How do you feel?)

description: Active verb meaning to hit, knock, or get injured.
examples: Χτυπάω την πόρτα. (I knock on the door.) / Χτύπησα το πόδι μου. (I hurt my foot.)

description: Active verb meaning to offer something to someone.
examples: Προσφέρω βοήθεια. (I offer help.) / Τι προσφέρει η εταιρεία; (What does the company offer?)

description: Active verb meaning to study or examine something carefully.
examples: Μελετώ για τις εξετάσεις. (I study for the exams.) / Μελετάει κάθε μέρα. (She studies every day.)

description: Active verb meaning to look at something.
examples: Κοιτάζω τη θάλασσα. (I look at the sea.) / Κοίτα εδώ! (Look here!)

description: Active verb meaning to lift or raise something. Also means to pick up (phone).
examples: Σηκώνω τη βαλίτσα. (I lift the suitcase.) / Σήκωσε το τηλέφωνο! (Pick up the phone!)

description: Active verb meaning to include or encompass something.
examples: Η τιμή συμπεριλαμβάνει πρωινό. (The price includes breakfast.)

description: Active verb meaning to belong to someone or be a member of something.
examples: Αυτό μου ανήκει. (This belongs to me.) / Ανήκω σε μια ομάδα. (I belong to a team.)

description: Active verb meaning to try or taste something.
examples: Δοκίμασε αυτό! (Try this!) / Δοκιμάζω ελληνικό φαγητό. (I try Greek food.)

description: Impersonal verb meaning it means or signifies. Third person form.
examples: Τι σημαίνει αυτό; (What does this mean?) / Σημαίνει πολλά για μένα. (It means a lot to me.)

description: Active verb meaning to call someone or invite/summon.
examples: Καλώ τον γιατρό. (I call the doctor.) / Σε καλώ στο πάρτι μου. (I invite you to my party.)

description: Active verb meaning to save or rescue someone or something.
examples: Σώζω ένα ζώο. (I save an animal.) / Σώσε τα αρχεία! (Save the files!)

description: Active verb meaning to think or believe (opinion). Less certain than ξέρω.
examples: Νομίζω ότι ναι. (I think so.) / Τι νομίζεις; (What do you think?)

description: Active verb meaning to change or alter something. Also intransitive.
examples: Αλλάζω ρούχα. (I change clothes.) / Τα πράγματα αλλάζουν. (Things change.)

description: Active verb meaning to send a message, package, etc.
examples: Στέλνω ένα μήνυμα. (I send a message.) / Στείλε μου φωτογραφίες! (Send me photos!)

description: Active verb meaning to open something like a door, window, or container.
examples: Ανοίγω το παράθυρο. (I open the window.) / Άνοιξε την πόρτα! (Open the door!)

description: Active verb meaning to return or come back to a place.
examples: Επιστρέφω σπίτι. (I return home.) / Πότε επιστρέφεις; (When are you returning?)

description: Active verb meaning to mean or intend something specific.
examples: Τι εννοείς; (What do you mean?) / Εννοώ ότι... (I mean that...)

description: Neuter plural noun meaning cutlery or silverware (literally knife-forks).
examples: Βάλε τα μαχαιροπίρουνα στο τραπέζι. (Put the cutlery on the table.)

description: Neuter noun (indeclinable) meaning radiator or central heating unit. From French 'calorifère'.
examples: Άναψε το καλοριφέρ. (Turn on the heater.) / Το καλοριφέρ δεν δουλεύει. (The radiator isn't working.)

description: Neuter noun meaning desk or office.
examples: Κάθομαι στο γραφείο μου. (I sit at my desk.) / Πάω στο γραφείο. (I go to the office.)

description: Neuter noun meaning carpet or rug.
examples: Ένα ωραίο χαλί. (A nice carpet.) / Καθάρισε το χαλί. (Clean the rug.)
